rationale_for_baseline_tier: This is an evolving systemic allergic (anaphylaxis-spectrum) reaction with cardiovascular involvement (presyncope, HR 112, BP 105/65) and active escalation, but with a currently patent airway and no adrenaline on site. It requires emergency in-person assessment now (ED, T4) with an immediate T5 (call 000 ambulance) trigger the moment any airway/breathing/collapse feature appears. Under-triage caution: because the reaction is escalating and can decompensate within minutes, many clinicians would argue directly for T5; T4 is the minimum defensible baseline and any airway sign mandates T5.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Dizziness/faintness worsening, inability to stand, feeling of impending collapse, or any worsening of the overall reaction
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately - do not wait to see if it settles
escalation_edges.rationale: Cardiovascular symptoms in an allergic reaction meet anaphylaxis criteria; worsening light-headedness signals impending shock and needs paramedic adrenaline within minutes.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel more faint, cannot stand, feel like you are going to pass out, or the reaction gets worse in any way, call 000 straight away and tell them it is a severe allergic reaction.
escalation_edges.contextual_modification: No adrenaline auto-injector is available at home, so paramedic response is the only route to adrenaline - call 000 rather than attempting to drive.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any swelling of the lips, tongue or throat, throat tightness, difficulty swallowing, noisy breathing (stridor), wheeze, or difficulty breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately at the first sign - airway involvement is time-critical
escalation_edges.rationale: Airway involvement is the most rapidly fatal feature of anaphylaxis; it is currently absent but can develop within minutes and requires immediate ambulance and adrenaline.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips, tongue or throat start to swell, your throat feels tight, you have trouble swallowing or breathing, or your breathing becomes noisy - call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: No adrenaline auto-injector available on site
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Removes the option of immediate self-treatment; strengthens the case for immediate 000/ED rather than home observation.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Only an anxious spouse present, potential for rapid deterioration
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the spouse knows exactly when and how to call 000 and to stay with the patient.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Arrange emergency in-person assessment now for an evolving allergic reaction; go to the nearest emergency department, and call 000 immediately for any airway, breathing or collapse features.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 for ambulance for red-flag features; otherwise immediate ED presentation.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This is an after-hours emergency presentation - do not wait for standard clinic hours; use 000/ED tonight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document suspected alpha-gal / anaphylaxis-spectrum reaction, vitals reported, treatment advised, and the need for specific-IgE testing and immunology referral.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating an evolving anaphylaxis-spectrum reaction as simple hives or gastroenteritis and advising home management / routine review.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Patient deteriorates into anaphylactic shock or airway compromise at home with no adrenaline available and no timely emergency response, risking death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising same-day urgent care rather than emergency assessment, underestimating the speed at which the reaction can decompensate.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling 000 immediately when airway is patent and cardiovascular signs are mild is defensible and only mildly over-triage; acceptable given under-triage is weighted 3x.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether this is 'just hives'/gastro or an anaphylaxis-spectrum reaction that will decompensate, and whether the trigger is truly alpha-gal.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the worst plausible scenario (anaphylaxis) as the working assumption: escalate for emergency assessment, safety-net for airway/collapse, and arrange confirmatory testing later.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the currently patent airway or the delayed onset to reassure the patient and defer urgent assessment.
